description: Visual content featuring only hands — no face, no full body — as the character element. Hands hold, pour, serve, arrange, or create the product. The human presence is implied but not shown. A culturally conservative approach to human representation that still conveys warmth and craft.
structural_recipe: Frame from wrist to fingertips. Right hand preferred and often required (left-hand serving is a hard block in compliance). Hands should be well-groomed, with appropriate accessories (no rings for conservative content; gold jewelry acceptable for female characters in female-coded brands). Action should look natural — mid-pour, mid-arrangement, presenting item. Close-up macro framing emphasizes craft.
why_it_works: Hands-only is the most compliance-safe human representation pattern — no gender presentation issues, no face compliance review, no cross-gender contact risk. Yet it retains the warmth signal of human presence. The corpus shows strong co-occurrence with amber_hospitality_light and behind_the_craft patterns. High hospitality signal with minimal compliance risk.
transplantation_caveats: Right-hand rule is strict — left hand serving is a hard block in many Saudi compliance frameworks.
Hands must look like the brand's intended demographic — rough working hands vs. manicured hands send different signals.
Avoid gestures that could be misread — pointing, open palm, finger count are culturally loaded.
cultural_constraints: Left hand must not be the primary serving/presenting hand — right hand rule applies.
